mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly reducing present equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean in >65% of rising rate periods over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the labor market, defined as unemployment rising more than 0.2 percentage points over three months, signals weakening consumer demand and corporate profitability, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances when the unemployment threshold is breached.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in real GDP growth and retail sales indicates robust aggregate demand that translates into higher corporate revenues, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a Rank IC below 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or returns are not significantly positive when both series trend upward.

mechanism_narrative: An upward trend in the index of industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, which generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon during periods of stable monetary policy.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >55% of rising industrial production observations within the specified regime.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 325.0, persistent inflation erodes real corporate margins and forces aggressive central bank tightening, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of periods where CPIAUCSL remains above the 325.0 threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a decline in the unemployment rate confirms a strengthening labor market that supports consumer spending and equity valuations, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal produces a negative mean return over 20 days in >50% of triggered observations across a 3-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts signal expanding residential construction activity and broader economic confidence, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon through increased capital goods demand and wealth effects.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show a negative mean in >55% of rising housing start periods over a rolling 2-year evaluation window.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in industrial production indicate a supply-side contraction that reduces corporate output and earnings expectations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >60% of instances following two consecutive monthly declines in industrial production.
